error.signature: Error: ENOENT: no such file or directory — Node.js path resolution fails with spaces

verdict.summary: Node.js or npm scripts fail when project directory contains spaces, commonly in Windows 'My Documents' or 'Program Files'.

dead_ends.why_fails: File system APIs expect OS paths, not URI-encoded strings; %20 is treated as literal characters

dead_ends.action: Replace spaces with %20 in package.json scripts
dead_ends.why_fails: Shell and Node resolve %20 as literal text, not as space replacement

workarounds.action: Move project to a path without spaces or create a junction/symlink
workarounds.how: mklink /J C:\project "C:\My Documents\project"  # Windows junction; ln -s on Linux

workarounds.action: Wrap all path references in double quotes in npm scripts and shell commands
